en: If you could fight anyone, who would you fight?
ko: 아무나 싸울 수 있다면, 누구랑 싸울 거야?

en: Yeah, why? Who would you fight? Fight my dad.
ko: 응, 왜? 너는 누구랑 싸울 건데?

en: I don't know my dad. I mean, I know him, but...
He left when I was, like, six years old. Married this other woman and had some other kids.
ko: 다른 여자랑 결혼하고 다른 애들도 낳았지.

en: And he, like, did this every six years. He goes to a new city and starts a new family.
Fucker setting up franchises. My dad never went to college.
So it was real important that I go. That sounds familiar.
So I graduate. I call him up long distance. I say, Dad, now what?
He says, get a job. Same here.
Now I'm 25. I make my yearly call again.
ko: 또 일 년에 한 번 전화를 걸지.

en: I say, Dad, now what? He says, I don't know. Get married.
ko: 아빠, 이제 뭐 해? 하니까, 모르겠다, 결혼해, 라고 하더라.

en: Dad, I mean... I can't get married.
I'm a 30-year-old boy. We're a generation of men raised by women.
I'm wondering if another woman is really the answer we need.
ko: 또 다른 여자가 정말 우리에게 필요한 답인지 모르겠어.
